macro_summary: Markets are navigating a fragile ceasefire aftermath — Wednesday's historic rally (Dow +1,320 points, per Yahoo Finance) on the US-Iran two-week ceasefire has now stalled, with Yahoo Finance reporting overnight that Iran claims the ceasefire was broken in under 24 hours. SPY trades at $676 (+0.35% daily, per fast_info), though the history-derived figure suggests a larger +2.5% move from prior close — note the data anomaly here; the fast_info headline figure is used for narrative. VIX has collapsed 18.3% to 21.04, its lowest reading in over a month, reflecting the ceasefire-driven risk-on wave, but the overnight ceasefire fracture introduces fresh uncertainty heading into Thursday's session. US10Y at 4.29% (-1.2%) edges closer to the 4.20% NIM reset threshold that would benefit Singapore banks. J.P. Morgan Asset Management, per their published outlook, projects CPI to drift from 2.8% toward 3.5% by Q4 before easing — a hawkish-for-longer backdrop that keeps rate cuts modest.
commodities_note: WTI shows a significant data anomaly: fast_info reports $97.03 (+0.57%), while history data implies a prior close of $112.95, suggesting an apparent -14.1% single-day move that conflicts with the 5-day trend. The fast_info headline ($97.03, +0.57%) is used as the reliable figure — consistent with Wednesday's ceasefire relief sell-off from the ~$115 peak. Oil has dropped roughly 22% from its Hormuz-crisis peak but remains elevated. Gold also shows a conflict: fast_info reports $4,755 (-1.51%) while history implies +2.1% from a prior close of $4,657 — fast_info is used as the canonical figure. Gold's pullback from its recent highs is notable; at $4,755 it remains at historically extreme levels. Despite the pullback, no attractive entry exists for gold-linked instruments as GLD is well extended above its 50-day average. SOXX data shows a similar anomaly (fast_info +2.5% vs history +6.5%); the +2.5% fast_info figure is used. DXY at 99.03 reflects reduced safe-haven demand on ceasefire optimism.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

D05_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. DBS sits 1.2% above its 50-day average of SGD 56.51, but the risk/reward here is structurally distorted — the nearest resistance at SGD 57.47 is only 0.5% away while invalidation (a break of the 50-day SMA) is 1.2% below, producing a 0.4:1 R:R that makes current levels unattractive for fresh entry. The tight stop warrants caution: normal intraday volatility could trigger the invalidation before the thesis is tested. Use the 200-day SMA at SGD 52.28 (8.6% below) as the mental stop for position sizing — that is the structural support that actually matters. US10Y at 4.29% is inching toward the 4.20% NIM reset threshold; a sustained break below would materially improve the earnings outlook. Volume is below average, which limits conviction. The PEG of 4.75 and -3.3% revenue growth flag this as a yield story (5.3% dividend), not a growth one — earnings are expected to decline 8.8%, which is modest but directionally negative. Bottom line: DBS offers a solid yield but the entry is poor at current levels. Wait for either a pullback toward the 50-day SMA or a confirmed US10Y break below 4.20%.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

O39_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — OCBC has reclaimed its 50-day SMA (now rising) and is the only Singapore bank with analyst consensus expecting positive earnings growth (+2.7%), distinguishing it from DBS and UOB. OCBC trades at SGD 22.20, 3.8% above its 50-day average of SGD 21.39 with RSI at 62 — extended but not overbought. The R:R of 1.4:1 (upside to SGD 23.31 resistance vs invalidation at SGD 21.39) is derived, not observed, so treat with appropriate skepticism. Structural support sits at SGD 18.58 (16.3% below) — a meaningful cushion if the thesis holds. At 12.4x forward PE, OCBC trades at a 10% discount to the Singapore cluster median, which is reasonable given 6.5% revenue growth — the best growth profile in the local bank cluster. The PEG of 3.79 is elevated but less relevant for a bank; price-to-book at 1.66x is moderate. Volume is below average, which limits conviction for entry now. US10Y at 4.29% still above the 4.20% reset threshold, so NIM pressure persists. Bottom line: OCBC is the best-positioned Singapore bank on fundamentals. Watch for a pullback to SGD 21.50-21.80 (near the 50-day SMA) on above-average volume as the preferred entry.

U11_SI.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. UOB at SGD 37.27 is essentially at its 50-day SMA (SGD 37.35, -0.2%), and the R:R is deeply distorted — resistance at SGD 37.40 is only 0.3% away while the 200-day SMA at SGD 35.67 (4.3% below) serves as the real downside reference, yielding a 0.1:1 ratio that makes current levels a non-starter for entry. The analyst consensus is HOLD with -6.7% expected earnings growth and -1.8% revenue growth — the weakest fundamentals in the Singapore bank cluster. At 10.2x forward PE (a 26% discount to cluster median), the market is pricing in this weakness. The 3.8% dividend provides some floor but is not compelling enough to override the deteriorating earnings trajectory. No action until US10Y breaks below 4.20% sustainably or UOB shows volume-confirmed support at the 200-day SMA of SGD 35.67. Upgrades to WATCH above SGD 39.49 with volume.

C6L_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from BUY to HOLD — the R:R has deteriorated and a 64.3% expected earnings decline makes the prior BUY signal untenable at current price levels. Yesterday's BUY at SGD 6.66 assumed improving conditions, but the analyst consensus now shows an expected earnings collapse of 64.3% — a material deterioration that must be weighed prominently. Per Reuters (April 7), Air India CEO Campbell Wilson has resigned amid persistent losses, a reminder of ongoing aviation sector stress. The ceasefire partially restores the airspace narrative, but Iran's claim of a broken ceasefire overnight undermines the thesis. The R:R of 1.2:1 (nearest resistance SGD 6.78 vs invalidation at the 200-day SMA SGD 6.56) is tight and observed, but the 64.3% earnings decline makes this a binary event play rather than a clean entry. The invalidation at SGD 6.56 is 1.5% below with structural support at SGD 6.45 (3.2% below). FCF yield at 8.2% is strong, and the 5.2% dividend is supportive, but a 64.3% earnings decline is incompatible with a BUY signal without a concrete reversal catalyst confirmed. Per the temporary-shock reversal exception: airspace closure costs drove the earnings decline, and a durable ceasefire would reverse this — but the ceasefire is fractured per Yahoo Finance overnight. Half-position sizing would be mandatory if re-entering, but the fractured ceasefire blocks the exception condition. Bottom line: SIA was worth a look on the ceasefire; the broken ceasefire overnight makes this a wait-and-see. Re-enters BUY territory if the ceasefire is confirmed durable and airspace fully reopens.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

Z74_SI.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to HOLD — the R:R is severely distorted (20:1 ratio from a 0.2% stop at the 50-day SMA of SGD 4.94), which automatically blocks any upgraded signal. The tight_invalidation flag and rr_distorted flag are both triggered: invalidation is just 0.2% below at SGD 4.94, while structural support sits at SGD 4.43 (10.5% below) — the more meaningful thesis test. Normal intraday volatility can trigger the invalidation before the thesis is tested. SingTel at SGD 4.95 is essentially glued to its 50-day SMA with RSI at 48 in neutral-bearish territory and volume below average. The forward PE of 24.5x is a 77.5% premium to the Singapore cluster median of 13.8x — expensive for 0.9% revenue growth. The PEG of 1.64x is elevated. The 2.6% dividend is modest. No conviction entry exists here. Upgrades to WATCH if price breaks above SGD 5.15 (nearest resistance) with volume, or on a pullback with a better-defined stop above structural support.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — NVDA has reclaimed the vicinity of its 50-day SMA ($182.22) at $182.08 (-0.08%), and the ceasefire-driven risk-on environment has improved the technical backdrop materially. Invalidation sits at the 200-day SMA at $180.32 (1.0% below); structural support at $177.41 (2.6% below) is the more meaningful thesis test — normal intraday vol could touch $180 without breaking the thesis. The nearest resistance is $184.47 (1.3% upside), producing a 1.3:1 R:R that is below the 2:1 threshold for ACCUMULATE — this is a WATCH, not a buy. NVDA's Blackwell Ultra ramp (per NVDA Q4 FY2026 call) with a Q1 FY2027 guide of $78B implies faster-than-expected revenue pull-forward, which is the core thesis. At 16.4x forward PE with a 0.72 PEG and 73.2% revenue growth — the cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to growth rate — fundamentals are compelling. Volume is normal (not confirming). The fractured Iran ceasefire introduces overnight risk. An IBD article (April 9) flagged Nvidia partner Vertiv among new buy signals, supporting sentiment. Bottom line: NVDA is attractively valued but needs to hold $180 and confirm volume before entry makes sense. Watch for a test of $177-180 support with above-average volume as the cleaner entry.

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to HOLD — AMD's fundamental thesis has strengthened materially (BofA raised its 2026 chips forecast to $1.3 trillion naming AMD a top driver, per Yahoo Finance April 8), and the pipeline upgraded the raw signal from CAUTION to HOLD. However, the entry block is hard and non-negotiable: AMD is 10.5% above its 50-day SMA of $209.81 with only 4 days above it and the SMA itself declining — the pipeline's BLOCKED designation is valid and stands. The 5-day move of +14% further triggers a momentum-chase block. The R:R is distorted at 0.1:1 (9.5% downside to invalidation, 0.9% upside to $234 resistance). The AMD-Meta framework agreement for MI450 deployments (AMD IR, Feb 24, 2026) remains the multi-year thesis anchor — a framework agreement ceiling, not a firm purchase order. At 21.5x forward PE with a PEG of 0.65 and 34.1% revenue growth, AMD is reasonably priced for its growth rate. Structural support at $198.35 (200-day SMA, 14.4% below) is the real thesis test. Bottom line: AMD's story is strong but the stock has run too far, too fast. Wait for a pullback toward $210-215 (50-day SMA zone) before considering entry.

INTC.entry_block: BLOCKED: +26.6% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel's involvement in Tesla's Terafab AI chip manufacturing initiative (per Simply Wall St., April 9) has driven an extraordinary +7.5% single session and +33.6% 5-day surge — INTC is now 26.6% above its 50-day SMA at an extreme extension that triggers a hard block with no exceptions. RSI at 72.2 is overbought. The pipeline's BLOCKED designation stands absolutely — no entry at these levels. Critically, INTC's 18A foundry thesis remains unproven: no confirmed external customers (internal products only, Panther Lake), FCF yield is negative (-1.52%), revenue is declining (-4.1%), and at 58.7x forward PE it trades at a 173% premium to the semis cluster median without a growth justification. The Terafab catalyst is potentially structural but far too new (announced today) to have graduated from geopolitical/event-driven to fundamental re-rating. Earnings are April 24 (15 days away) — a binary event that adds further risk. Bottom line: Intel's price has moved; the fundamental story has not changed enough to justify entry after a 34% surge. Wait for a meaningful pullback and 18A external customer confirmation before revisiting.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to BUY — ASML has reclaimed its 50-day SMA ($1,391) for the first time this week, closing +4.3% at $1,421 on ceasefire-driven risk-on with above-average volume confirming the move. Invalidation is at the 50-day SMA of $1,391.24 (2.1% below); structural support at $1,283.60 (9.7% below) is the deeper thesis test. The R:R is 2.4:1 (upside to $1,491 resistance vs 2.1% downside) with observed quality — this is the only clean BUY entry on the watchlist. ASML earnings are April 15 (6 days away — within the 7-day earnings proximity window), which is a binary risk that warrants flagging: entering ahead of Q1 results carries event risk. The MATCH Act proposal (per Simply Wall St., April 8) targeting ASML's China DUV exposure is a legislative risk, but no law has passed. At 32.5x forward PE with a PEG of 2.18 and only 4.9% revenue growth, ASML is not cheap for its current growth rate — the premium is justified by its EUV monopoly and High-NA ramp (per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call). However, note: RSI is 56.7, entry is 2.1% above the 50-day SMA (slightly extended), and volume confirms. Per ASML Q4 2025 earnings call, the High-NA EUV customer ramp for Intel 14A starts H2 2026 — that is the growth re-rating event. Bottom line: ASML is the cleanest technical entry today, but earnings in 6 days make this a half-position at most. Enter $1,390-1,421, stop below $1,391 (50-day SMA). Full commitment after April 15 if earnings hold.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

MU.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — MU's technical picture has improved dramatically (+20.4% in 5 days), reclaiming its 50-day SMA, but the 5-day momentum block is hard: a +20.4% surge in a week triggers the pipeline's momentum-chase block and entry is blocked regardless of fundamentals. The R:R is distorted (8.4:1 looks attractive but is driven by a 0.9% stop at the 50-day SMA — rr_distorted is true). Structural support at $357.52 (12.1% below) is the meaningful reference. MU's Micron New York megafab plans and SiMa.ai edge AI investment (per Simply Wall St., April 9) reinforce the ecosystem-building narrative. The forward PE of 4.1x vs trailing 17.8x signals an enormous expected earnings inflection — flagged here as an indicator of anticipated recovery, not normal operation. The 196% revenue growth and 756% expected earnings growth are inference-demand driven (HBM/KV cache scaling), not training — a distinction that matters for sustainability. MU CEO projections about memory price increases carry inherent seller bias and should not be taken at face value. The PEG of 0.21 appears anomalous given forward PE of 4.1x — verify earnings growth estimate before citing as a primary valuation argument. Bottom line: MU's story is compelling but the stock has moved too fast. Wait for consolidation back toward $380-405 (50-day SMA zone) before entering.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal changed from HOLD to WATCH — AMZN at $221.25 has reclaimed its 50-day SMA ($213.72), is 3.5% above it with RSI at 60.7, and shows above-average volume supporting the move. The raw signal upgraded to BUY, but the R:R of 1.9:1 (upside to $235.31 resistance vs 3.4% downside to the 50-day SMA) falls just below the 2:1 threshold for ACCUMULATE. Additionally, RSI at 60.7 is approaching the 60 gate. The $200B AI capex announcement (per AMZN Q4 2025 earnings call) drove a -10% selloff earlier — the market's concern is timing of capex-to-earnings conversion. AWS growth remains the core thesis, with custom Trainium chips building competitive moat. At 23.6x forward PE with 13.6% revenue growth, AMZN is fairly valued — PEG of 1.61 is neutral. FCF yield at 1.0% is low; the thesis depends on future earnings conversion. An analyst cited by Motley Fool (April 9) projects up to 45% upside, though single-analyst price targets warrant appropriate skepticism. Bottom line: AMZN is fairly priced for what you're getting from AWS, but not quite cheap enough for a clean entry today. Becomes more actionable on a pullback toward the 50-day SMA at $213-215.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — Google has reclaimed its 50-day SMA ($308.52) for the first time in over two weeks, closing at $314.74 (+0.64%) on 9.72% gains over 5 days. However, the R:R of 0.7:1 (upside to $319.23 resistance vs 2.0% downside to the 50-day SMA invalidation) is unfavourable for entry now. IDB flagged Google among 7 new buy signals post-ceasefire (April 9). The TPU v7 dominance thesis (Google Cloud Next 2025 keynote) — with Meta renting compute time and the canceled Olympus chip project validating TPU superiority — is the fundamental driver. However, the PEG of 2.21 and forward PE of 23.4x are not cheap for 18% revenue growth. FCF yield at 1.0% is low. Volume is normal (not elevated), which limits conviction. The TurboQuant algorithm disruption narrative (Day 14) remains a latent risk — if Google's own efficiency gains reduce external GPU demand, this indirectly affects the capex ecosystem GOOG benefits from. Bottom line: Google has improved technically but the near-term entry is poor at current levels. Becomes actionable on a pullback toward $308-310 (50-day SMA) with volume.

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. LITE is 40.4% above its 50-day SMA — an extreme extension that triggers a hard block with no exceptions at any level. The sma50_warning is explicit: a pullback to the 50-day SMA would require a crash-level -29% move. Use shorter-term supports: $642.37 (26.6% below) and $573.73 (36% below). The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA investor.nvidia.com press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion (March 9, 2026) drove the 1,400%+ YTD run — per learned heuristics, S&P inclusion buying typically exhausts within 3-5 sessions of effective date, suggesting the inclusion bid may be largely absorbed. FCF yield is essentially zero (-0.03%), and trailing PE of 238x makes the PEG of 0.18 anomalous — verify earnings growth estimate before citing. The forward PE of 56.8x is a 19.8% premium to the Photonics cluster median. No entry exists at current levels regardless of the NVDA partnership thesis. COHR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Coherent is 15.4% above its 50-day SMA of $244.16 — a hard entry block that stands. The NVDA $2B strategic investment (confirmed via NVIDIA newsroom press release, March 2, 2026) and S&P 500 inclusion provide a structural foundation the pipeline cannot dismiss. Datacenter revenues at 72% of sales with 73% earnings growth expected (per Zacks, April 8) make COHR fundamentally interesting. The PEG of 0.07 appears anomalous — a PEG below 0.2 with a 38x forward PE requires verification of the underlying earnings growth estimate; do not cite as a primary valuation argument. FCF yield at 0.25% is negligible, meaning the thesis depends entirely on earnings growth materializing. The R:R of 0.5:1 (downside 13.4%, upside 6.5% to $300 resistance) is unfavourable at current prices. Volume confirms the move (above average). Structural support at $230.15 (18.3% below) is the real entry zone. Bottom line: COHR has the right catalysts but has moved too far from any rational entry. Revisit on a pullback toward $245-255 (near the 50-day SMA) where the thesis can be entered with appropriate risk management.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

XLE.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — XLE at $58.05 is 3.7% above its 50-day SMA ($55.96, rising), with RSI at 48.5 in neutral-bearish territory and above-average volume confirming yesterday's bounce. The R:R of 2.6:1 (upside to $63.46 resistance vs 3.6% downside to the 50-day SMA invalidation) is observed and favourable. XLE spent 69 consecutive sessions above the 50-day SMA — a structural trend supported by the Hormuz oil crisis. However, the ceasefire (however fragile) has materially changed the near-term oil narrative: WTI at $97 is well below the $115 peak, and if the ceasefire holds, the energy premium unwinds further. MT Newswires reported the NYSE Energy Sector Index fell 3.3-4.4% Wednesday afternoon — headwinds were building even before the ceasefire fracture news. As an ETF, standard PEG/FCF checks don't apply. Structural support at $46.75 (200-day SMA, 19.5% below) provides a long-term floor. Bottom line: XLE is technically improving but the oil price thesis is binary on ceasefire durability. Actionable at $55.50-56.50 (near the 50-day SMA) if oil stabilizes above $90. Avoid chasing the current bounce.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 76x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal changed from WATCH to CAUTION — Palantir fell 9.1% on heavy volume yesterday, breaking below its 50-day SMA ($145.62) to $140.76, driven by valuation concerns and Michael Burry's public critique (per Motley Fool, April 8) plus UK regulatory scrutiny. The prior WATCH signal was predicated on technical resilience; that resilience has now broken. At 75.6x forward PE with a 2.94 PEG — a 220% premium to the BigTech cluster median of 23.6x — valuation is extremely stretched and is incompatible with a WATCH or higher signal per framework rules without very specific growth justification. The $10B Army IDIQ framework ceiling (indefinite delivery/indefinite quantity — not firm revenue) and $448M Navy ShipOS deal support the defense AI thesis, but analyst price target upgrades (Rosenblatt $200, Daiwa Buy/$180) are bullish calls from buy-side firms. The R:R of 1.5:1 (upside to $161.92 resistance vs 10.3% downside to $126.30 support) is below the 2:1 threshold. FCF yield at 0.37% is negligible. Revenue growth at 70% is strong but the 223x trailing PE and 76x forward PE are hard to justify even with a 648% expected earnings growth figure. Bottom line: PLTR's defense AI thesis is real but the stock is egregiously expensive and has just broken a key technical level. Avoid until either valuation corrects significantly or the price reclaims $145.62 on volume.

VUAA_L.signal_rationale: Signal changed from CAUTION to WATCH — VUAA.L at $130.34 (+3.17%) has essentially reclaimed its 50-day SMA ($130.41, -0.06%), and the ceasefire-driven risk-on has materially improved the broad market backdrop with VIX dropping 18.3% to 21.04. The R:R of 2.3:1 (upside to $135.40 resistance vs 1.7% downside to the $128.13 support invalidation) is observed and technically favourable, though the tight_invalidation flag means normal volatility could trigger the stop. Use the 200-day SMA at $127.66 (2.1% below) as the structural mental stop. Volume is below average, which limits conviction. The ceasefire fracture overnight adds uncertainty. As an S&P 500 ETF, valuation metrics (trailing PE 26.9x) simply reflect broad market pricing — no PEG or FCF analysis applies. The overnight ceasefire news is the key risk: if the fracture escalates back toward active conflict, VIX reverts higher and this WATCH reverts to CAUTION quickly. Bottom line: VUAA.L is approaching a clean entry zone for broad market exposure, but the ceasefire situation must stabilize. Enter near $128-130 (50-day SMA support) if the geopolitical picture holds.

singapore.summary: The Singapore cluster shows a modest intraday split: US10Y at 4.29% is edging toward the 4.20% NIM reset threshold that would lift bank earnings, but has not broken through sustainably. OCBC (upgraded to WATCH) stands out as the only bank with positive earnings growth consensus (+2.7%) and a rising 50-day SMA — the divergence from DBS and UOB is the key cluster insight. SIA's signal was downgraded to HOLD as the fractured ceasefire undermines the airspace reopening thesis that drove yesterday's BUY.
singapore.thesis_status: Structurally impaired — NIM reset conditions near but unmet
singapore.key_development: OCBC diverges positively from cluster peers with rising SMA50 and positive earnings growth; SIA's ceasefire-dependent thesis fractured overnight

semiconductors.summary: SOXX +2.49% to $370.40 (fast_info basis) reflects broad semis relief, but the cluster is deeply split: ASML reclaimed its 50-day SMA and is the only clean BUY, while INTC is in extreme extension (+26.6% above SMA50) on Terafab news that has not yet translated to fundamental re-rating. MU and AMD remain entry-blocked from 5-day momentum surges. NVDA upgraded to WATCH as it holds the SMA50/200 range but lacks near-term R:R for entry.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Mixed — technical improvement but entry conditions met only for ASML
semiconductors.key_development: ASML reclaims 50-day SMA with volume ahead of April 15 earnings; INTC's Terafab involvement drives extreme extension without fundamental confirmation

big_tech.summary: The BigTech cluster has upgraded technically — AMZN and GOOG both reclaimed their 50-day SMAs in the ceasefire rally — but neither offers a clean entry with R:R above 2:1 at current prices. PLTR's 9.1% drop on heavy volume breaks the cluster in two: while AMZN and GOOG improve, PLTR deteriorates into confirmed CAUTION on valuation and technical breakdown simultaneously.
big_tech.thesis_status: Diverging — AMZN/GOOG improving, PLTR breaking down
big_tech.key_development: PLTR breaks below its 50-day SMA on Michael Burry critique and UK regulatory scrutiny, with valuation at 76x forward PE now the primary entry blocker

photonics.summary: Both LITE and COHR remain technically blocked from entry — LITE at 40.4% above SMA50 (extreme extension) and COHR at 15.4% (hard block). The NVDA $2B investments in both (confirmed NVIDIA IR, March 2, 2026) are structural, but the distance from any rational entry point means the cluster warrants watching, not buying, until a meaningful consolidation occurs.
photonics.key_development: No new developments; both remain hard-blocked pending meaningful pullbacks toward intermediate support levels

energy.summary: XLE upgraded to WATCH as the technical setup improves (2.6:1 R:R with volume) but the thesis hinges entirely on ceasefire durability — WTI at $97 is 22% below its Hormuz-crisis peak and the overnight ceasefire fracture reintroduces binary risk. The 69 consecutive sessions above SMA50 establish structural trend integrity, but an energy premium unwind scenario puts the SMA50 test at $55.96 as the critical level.
energy.thesis_status: Conditionally improving — binary on ceasefire durability
energy.key_development: Iran's claim of broken ceasefire overnight reintroduces upside oil risk that was briefly priced out; XLE's SMA50 at $55.96 is the key support to watch

index.summary: VUAA.L upgraded to WATCH as VIX collapses 18.3% to 21.04 and the ETF reclaims its 50-day SMA. The ceasefire fracture is the primary overnight risk — if VIX reverts above 25, the WATCH reverts to CAUTION. The near-term setup (2.3:1 R:R) is technically sound but confidence depends on geopolitical stabilization.
index.key_development: VIX at 21.04 (18-month low in this cycle) signals broad risk appetite recovery, but the overnight ceasefire fracture threatens to reverse this quickly

interconnected.name: Chevron (CVX)
interconnected.reason: Direct proxy for WTI oil price direction — the XLE WATCH signal's binary ceasefire thesis is best stress-tested against CVX as the largest integrated oil holding in XLE.
interconnected.entry_note: Becomes more interesting if WTI stabilizes above $95 after the ceasefire situation resolves; watch for a 5-7% pullback in CVX from current levels.

active_risks: Iran-Hormuz: Two-week ceasefire announced April 8, but Iran claimed ceasefire broken within 24 hours per Yahoo Finance overnight — Strait reopening timeline remains uncertain, WTI at $97
MATCH Act (US Congress): Proposed legislation would tighten export controls on ASML's DUV systems to China, expanding restrictions beyond existing EUV limits
US-China tech tariffs: Section 122 surcharges (15%, 150-day window) active; China counter-tariffs at 34% plus export controls on critical minerals remain in force
Federal Reserve hawkish floor: Fed holds at 3.50%-3.75%, US10Y at 4.29%, compressing tech multiples and keeping Singapore bank NIM under pressure
geopolitical.market_impact: The fractured ceasefire is showing up in energy — WTI at $97 is caught between Hormuz-closure upside risk and reopening relief downside, making XLE a binary bet rather than a clean WATCH. PLTR's 9.1% drop reflects valuation vulnerability when geopolitical tailwinds (defense spending boom from the Iran war) are offset by domestic scrutiny, while ASML's 4.3% gain shows the market is pricing in reduced chip-trade risk from de-escalation despite the MATCH Act threat.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Iran claimed the US-Iran ceasefire was broken in under 24 hours per Yahoo Finance overnight, reversing the prior session's historic 1,300+ point Dow rally and reintroducing Hormuz closure risk; Intel announced involvement in Tesla's Terafab AI chip manufacturing initiative.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Hold cash selectively — enter ASML as the single clean BUY with a half position ahead of April 15 earnings; avoid energy and broad market adds until ceasefire status clarifies.

events_this_week.event: ASML Q1 2026 Earnings — 6 days out; entering ahead of this binary event limits position size. MATCH Act overhang adds event risk.
Intel Q1 2026 Earnings — Terafab involvement and 18A yield progress will be key focus; stock is already at extreme extension making the setup binary.
Palantir Q1 2026 Earnings — contract validation for $10B Army IDIQ and Navy ShipOS deal critical for thesis; current CAUTION signal could upgrade on strong defense bookings.
AMD MI450 first shipments to Meta (H2 2026 per AMD IR) — first 1GW deployment under the multi-year framework agreement; performance warrant milestone.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: The fractured US-Iran ceasefire is the dominant overnight risk: a reversion to active conflict would collapse oil-relief trades (XLE), re-pressure aviation (SIA), and spike VIX above 25 — reverting multiple upgraded signals back to CAUTION in a single session.

consider_adding.note: Cleanest BUY on the watchlist — volume confirms the 50-day SMA reclaim. Half position ahead of April 15 earnings; full commitment post-results if thesis holds.

watch_for_entry.note: Holding 50-day/200-day SMA confluence. Actionable on a pullback to $177-180 with volume; R:R improves to 2:1+ at that level.

AWS thesis intact; R:R improves on pullback. Actionable below $215 (near 50-day SMA); not worth chasing at $221.
Binary on ceasefire durability. Actionable at $55.50-56.50 (50-day SMA) if WTI holds above $90. Avoid current bounce.
TPU dominance thesis intact but R:R unfavourable now. Actionable on pullback to $308-310 (50-day SMA) with volume.
HBM/inference demand thesis strong but 5-day momentum block hard. Wait for consolidation to $380-405 zone before entry.

Avoid. 40.4% above 50-day SMA (extreme extension hard block); FCF yield near zero. No entry regardless of NVDA partnership thesis.
Avoid at current levels. 15.4% above 50-day SMA with hard entry block. Revisit on pullback to $245-255.

hold_no_action.note: Downgraded from BUY — 64.3% expected earnings decline and fractured ceasefire block the temporary-shock reversal exception. Upgrades to BUY on confirmed durable ceasefire and airspace reopening.

Poor R:R at current levels (0.4:1). Upgrades to more attractive entry if US10Y breaks 4.20% or price pulls back to SGD 56.00-56.50.
Distorted R:R from 0.2% stop. No action until price confirms above SGD 5.15 with volume or pulls back to a better-defined entry.
Hard entry block at 10.5% above SMA50. Upgrades to WATCH on pullback toward $210-215 (50-day SMA). MI450 thesis intact for H2.
